name: Plaza Bakery's Cream Cheese Topped Chocolate Brownies
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Bitter or Semisweet Chocolate, chopped
recipeInstructions:
For the topping: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Lightly grease a 9 inch square baking pan. The pan must be at least 2 inches deep. Dust the pan with flour; tap out excess. Using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese and sugar in a medium bowl until smooth. Blend in egg, vanilla extract, and salt. Set the topping aside while preparing the brownie batter.
For the Brownies: Melt chocolate and butter in a heavy saucepan over very low heat, stirring until smooth. Cool slightly. Using an electric mixer, beat eggs, sugar, vanilla extract, and salt in a large bowl until thick, about 3 minutes. Stir in melted chocolate, then flour and semi-sweet chocolate chips. Pour the batter into the prepared pan; smooth the top. Spoon the cream cheese topping into a pastry bag fitted with a 1/2 inch round tip. Pipe the cheese mixture in diagonal lines atop the brownie batter, spacing each line 1/2 to 3/4 inches apart. Run the tip of a small knife through the cream cheese on opposite diagonals, spacing them 1/2 inch apart and creating a V pattern. Bake until a toothpick inserted comes out with a few moist crumbs still attached, about 40 minutes. Cool the dessert in the pan on a rack for 3 hours. Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours. It can be prepared up to 2 days ahead. Run a sharp knife around the pan to loosen. Cut the dessert into 12 brownies. Serve cold.
